sb_prompt: DISCUSS HOW YOU AND THE OTHER CALLER KEEP UP ON CURRENT EVENTS.      DO YOU GET MOST OF YOUR NEWS FROM TVOR PEOPLE YOU KNOW?  ARE YOU SATISFIED WITH THE QUALITY OF COVERAGE?
raw_output:
ParticipantA: How do you keep up with current events?
ParticipantB: I mostly get my news from social media and the internet.
ParticipantA: Yeah, same here. I try to stay up to date with what's going on in the world.
ParticipantB: Have you been satisfied with the quality of coverage lately?
ParticipantA: Honestly, it depends on the source. I think some outlets do a better job than others.
ParticipantB: Definitely. I think it's important to have multiple sources to get a well-rounded understanding of what's happening.
ParticipantA: Absolutely. And I also try to talk to people I know who have different perspectives.
ParticipantB: Yeah, I think that's a good idea. Sometimes you can learn a lot from different viewpoints.
ParticipantA: Agreed. It's important to have a diverse understanding of the news.
ParticipantB: Have you noticed any bias in the coverage you've seen?
ParticipantA: Yeah, I think most outlets have some form of bias. It's important to be aware of that and try to find sources that are more balanced.
ParticipantB: Absolutely. And I think it's also important to be critical of what you're reading and watching.
ParticipantA: Yeah, it's easy to be swayed by sensational headlines or clickbait.
ParticipantB: Exactly. It's important to be informed and not just rely on what we're fed.
ParticipantA: Definitely. And it's important to stay engaged and active in the world around us.
ParticipantB: Absolutely. It's our responsibility to be informed and make a difference.
ParticipantA: Well said. It's been great talking with you.
ParticipantB: Likewise, it was great chatting with you.
